items.title: Technical Program Lead
items.org: Family & Medical Leave Insurance (FAMLI), Maryland Department of Labor
items.description: Third hire to the Digital Service team building out a greenfield digital-first service to support Maryland workers and employers in the rollout of paid family and medical leave for the state. Initially hired as tech lead for front-end and accessibility, then promoted to lead the overall team of 17 state employees with expertise in product leadership, service design, content strategy, engineering, accessibility, and devops and oversee our agile vendor contracts worth $15.6 million annually.
leadership: Shaped program strategy for a complex, multi-year initiative and led inclusive, consultative processes that built shared understanding across technical, policy, and leadership stakeholders
Designed and owned the planning cadence, tracked open decisions and commitments across teams, and ensured senior stakeholders had consistent, transparent progress updates
Guided the team through rapid scaling and structural change, communicating proactively about shifting priorities and adjusting course as the program evolved
Scaled team of product, engineering, and design professionals from 3 to 17, including developing hiring strategy and processes and onboarding systems
Coached and managed 9 senior members of the Digital Services team, including other managers
technical: Led build vs. buy strategy and oversaw $15.6M in annual vendor contracts, coordinating cross-functional alignment and partnering with state cybersecurity governance on foundational decisions around tech stack, tooling, and third-party tools/services
Established standards and documentation for the engineering team, specifically around accessibility and front-end performance
culture: Built team operating rhythms for a hybrid, cross-functional team, including meeting structure and async documentation practices that supported team health as we scaled

items.title: Engineering Manager, Data Integrations
items.description: Founded in 2012, Panorama Education helps students achieve academic success and well-being and gives schools and school districts a platform to hear what families, students, and teachers need in the classroom.
leadership: Led teams through significant organizational shifts, including new team structures and revised processes, treating team feedback as a primary signal for when and how to adjust
Owned performance management for direct reports, including setting clear goals and expectations, conducting regular 1:1s and reviews, and creating conditions for engineers to do their best work
Refined the engineering career ladder and promotion process, creating a system that gave engineers clear, equitable paths to growth
Coached and developed mainly senior engineers, including facilitating promotions for 4 engineers over 3 quarters
technical: Partnered with product and design leads to own strategy and delivery across 3 cross-functional teams, setting clear goals, maintaining delivery health, and ensuring impact for users in 1,500+ school systems nationwide
Refined how the engineering team supported customer support requests, including improvements to our cross-functional communication and tools
culture: Rebooted a dormant affinity group for women, gender-nonconforming and nonbinary employees, providing a point of connection and support for team members

items.title: Engineering Manager, Front-end
items.description: Founded in 2012, Lumen Learning uses open educational resources (OER) to provide affordable course materials that improve learning and replace expensive textbooks in high-enrollment college courses. Over 400 institutions across the U.S. use Lumen's award-winning digital courseware for in-person, online, and blended courses.
leadership: Collaborated closely with product managers, developers, and designer to scope and estimate work to achieve our product roadmap

Designed the engineering career framework from scratch — defining leveling criteria, role expectations, and growth pathways to provide the team clarity and the organization a foundation for equitable performance management
Scaled front-end team from 2 to 4 engineers, building hiring and onboarding processes rigorous and successful enough that the broader engineering org adopted them as standard practice
Identified and reconciled fragmented team workflows, establishing consistent practices across the team
Coached 5 team members from junior to senior levels
technical: Provided guidelines to developers and content team for creating inclusive and accessible markup and about testing with screen readers
Writing documentation and participating in code review
culture: Conducted company-wide workshop on accessibility and inclusive design principles
Established and modeled engineering team norms, specifically around learning, professional development, and employee wellness

items.title: Adjunct Professor
responsibilities: Instructing undergraduate students in GD 220 (Graphic Design 2) and GD 431 (Front End Web development)
Developing curriculum and assignments for each 16-week course
Working 1:1 with students and providing detailed code review in response to assignments

items.title: Front-end Lead + Manager
items.description: Fractured Atlas builds software and provides services to help over 500,000 artists and arts organizations handle the business side of their work, like raising money, tracking donors and selling event tickets.
leadership: Served as project lead for crowdfunding and fundraising tool (over $25 million annually)
Managed UX engineering team, including holding 1:1s and setting team priorities
technical: Provided coding guidelines for writing maintainable Sass and inclusive and accessible markup
Built and deployed design system to establish a consistent look and feel across a variety of applications
Created site wireframes and prototypes to improve internal discussions and planning about site features and organization
culture: Developed and presented all-staff training on accessibility testing and best practices
Provided guidance on the organization's transition to being wholly virtual/remote (including being featured on our blog)

items.title: Superpowered Front-end Theming with Sass & SMACSS Workshop
National Women In Tech Nonprofit
items.description: Baltimore chapter leader and teacher from 2013-2017. Organized and presented hands-on workshops for women to learn web and software development. Coordinated teachers and volunteer teaching assistants across a wide variety of tech subjects. Built relationships with local companies and nonprofits who can provide space for our classes or other sponsorship opportunities. At the time I stepped down, the chapter had 1500+ members and had hosted 60+ events.
